reasoning: BC Hydro explicitly removed Tesla from its EV charger rebate program on March 12, 2025, to combat US tariffs and preference Canadian goods. As of late May 2025, the US-Canada trade war was still ongoing under new PM Mark Carney, although tensions were slightly de-escalating (with Canada pausing some counter-tariffs mid-month and a May 29 US court decision striking down parts of Trump's tariff agenda). Despite this de-escalation, reversing retaliatory policies at the provincial and administrative level usually takes time. Given the extremely short window (roughly one month) between these late-May developments and the July 1 resolution date, it is probable that BC Hydro will not update its eligibility lists fast enough to reinstate Tesla, especially since the initial ban was also characterized as politically targeting Elon Musk. Therefore, I estimate a 72% chance the exclusion remains on the BC Hydro website by July 2025.
